text.title: Continuous Discovery Habits Core Rules
text.summary: Weekly customer-discovery cadence where the Product Trio (PM, Design, Tech) maintains an Opportunity Solution Tree with verbatim-quote provenance and agent-assisted triage / coding / synthesis.

rule.statement: Every roadmap item MUST link to one OST opportunity, and that opportunity MUST cite ≥1 verbatim quote with participant_id + interview_date. Solution-shaped opportunities ('build X') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Solution-shaped opportunities collapse the need/answer distinction; quote provenance keeps the tree grounded in past behaviour.

rule.statement: PM MUST conduct ≥1 customer interview per calendar week; sampling diversity (segment, recency, plan-tier, churn-status) is enforced before each round.
rule.rationale: Weekly cadence is the keystone habit; diversity prevents power-user bias and surfaces undersampled segments.

rule.statement: One outcome per OST per quarter. Two outcomes in scope → build two trees and surface the cross-tree dependency explicitly.
rule.rationale: Merged outcomes create metric ambiguity and untestable assumptions.

rule.statement: Every opportunity links to ≥1 quote_id with participant + date. Synthesis agents consume full transcripts and verbatim quotes, never paraphrases.
rule.rationale: Paraphrased evidence is lossy and drifts the OST toward solution-shaped opportunities.

rule.statement: Opportunities with no new evidence in 60 days get parked; archive at 180 days. Tree health beats tree completeness.
rule.rationale: Unmaintained opportunities decay into dumping-ground noise and make the tree unusable past 200 nodes.

rule.statement: Lock the Trio to one meeting per week and ship a trio-prep doc 24h before. Without prep, the meeting collapses to a status update.
rule.rationale: Prep shifts the meeting from 'what to talk about' to 'which three opportunities are ready for the next solution test'.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — discovery already covered by customer-development or jobs-to-be-done; B2B < 20 logos with 12-month sales cycle; mature growth-stage where experimentation-at-scale is primary), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
